results.rendered_html:
sagh (cf. √sah) cl. 5. P. ( Dhātup. xxvii, 20 ) saghnoti (occurring only in impf. asaghnoḥ asaghnoḥ˚not Pot. saghnuyāt Subj. saghat, Prec. saghyāsam, and inf. sagdhyai; Gr. also pf. sasāgha aor. asaghīt, or asāghīt &c.),
to take upon one's self, be able to bear, be a match for (acc.),  RV. ;  TS. ;  MaitrS. ;  TĀr. ;
to hurt, injure, kill,  Dhātup.

results.rendered_html: √sagh , saghnoti  DHĀTUP. 27,20  (hiṃsāyām). saghat; wohl verwandt mit sah auf sich nehmen, zu tragen vermögen: asaghnorbhāram  ṚV. 1,31,3 . nahi tvadanyo girvaṇo giraḥ saghat (= prāpnoti  SĀY. ) 57,4 . aśvinostvā bāhubhyāṃ sadhyāsam den Soma  TS. 3,2,5,1 .
